channels.name: #engineering
channels.description: Core game server and client engineering discussions
channels.style: Technical, code-oriented, direct. Frequent use of Go/Rust/CockroachDB terminology. Discussions about ECS architecture, tick rates, networking, and performance. Participants share code snippets, debug logs, and profiling results.

channels.name: #game-design
channels.description: Game mechanics, class balance, content design
channels.style: Creative but analytical. Mixes narrative flavor with numbers and formulas. Discussions about class balance, ability tuning, rift mechanics, and player progression. References game design documents and playtest data.

channels.name: #art-direction
channels.description: Visual design, environment art, character concepts
channels.style: Visual and evocative. Heavy use of artistic terminology, reference images, and mood descriptions. Discussions about zone aesthetics, character designs, VFX, and the cyberpunk-meets-nature art style.

channels.name: #lore-narrative
channels.style: Narrative-driven, imaginative. In-depth discussions about faction politics, NPC motivations, quest chains, and world history. Sometimes includes in-character dialogue drafts and lore document excerpts.

channels.name: #devops-infra
channels.style: Operational, concise, urgent during incidents. Discussions about Kubernetes clusters, Agones game server allocation, CockroachDB cluster health, monitoring dashboards, and deployment pipelines.

channels.name: #general
channels.style: Casual and friendly. Mix of studio announcements, cross-team questions, social chat, and quick coordination. Lighter tone than specialty channels.
